title: Billing management page: current plan, connection count, upgrade CTA
description: Build the billing management UI at /dashboard/settings. Show the user's current subscription tier, their connection count, the pricing tier table, and an Upgrade button that triggers the Stripe Checkout flow from TASK-10. Also update the UpgradePromptModal from TASK-8 to use the actual Stripe checkout instead of a placeholder link.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /dashboard/settings shows: current plan badge (Free / Growth / Scale), current connection count, and a pricing table
Pricing table shows: Free (≤3 connections, $0), Growth (4–15, $19/mo), Scale (16+, $39/mo)
On a free plan, Upgrade buttons appear next to Growth and Scale tiers
Clicking Upgrade calls POST /api/stripe/checkout with the correct priceId and redirects to Stripe Checkout
On a paid plan, the current plan row is highlighted; a 'Manage subscription' link appears
After successful Stripe checkout redirect back (?success=true), a success toast is shown
UpgradePromptModal's Upgrade button now calls POST /api/stripe/checkout instead of navigating to settings
tsc reports no new type errors
Playwright screenshot shows the billing page with pricing table

steps.details: Read src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx to understand current content. Identify where to add billing UI.

steps.description: Add billing section to settings page
steps.details: Add a 'Billing' Card section to the settings page. Query the current user's subscription_tier from the users table (via AuthContext or a direct Supabase query). Query the connection count using getConnectedCompanyCount from TASK-7. Display both.

Create or inline a pricing table with three rows: Free, Growth, Scale. Each row shows: tier name, connection limit, price, and an Upgrade button (disabled/hidden if already on that tier or higher). Use a highlighted border on the current plan row.

steps.description: Wire Upgrade buttons to Stripe Checkout
steps.details: Upgrade button onClick: async () => { const res = await fetch('/api/stripe/checkout', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({ priceId: STRIPE_PRICE_GROWTH, userId: user.id }) }); const { url } = await res.json(); window.location.href = url; }. Use NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PRICE_GROWTH and NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PRICE_SCALE env vars for price IDs (or hardcode them as constants for now).
Read URL search params on the settings page. If ?success=true is present, show a toast: 'Subscription activated! Your plan has been upgraded.' Remove the param from the URL after showing.

steps.description: Update UpgradePromptModal to call Stripe Checkout
steps.details: In src/components/dashboard/UpgradePromptModal.tsx, replace the navigate-to-settings logic on the Upgrade button with the same Stripe Checkout fetch call from step 4.
Run npx tsc --noEmit and npx eslint --fix. Start dev server. Navigate to /dashboard/settings. Take screenshot .agent/screenshots/TASK-11-billing.png. Verify pricing table renders.

technicalNotes: Price IDs should be in environment variables — NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for client-side use
The 'Manage subscription' link for paid users can link to the Stripe Customer Portal (stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create) — skip this for now, a placeholder is fine
subscription_tier in the users table is updated by the Stripe webhook — after checkout redirect, it may take a few seconds to update; show a loading state
